title: Okta Identity Provider
description: Okta as an enterprise-grade OIDC/OAuth2 identity provider for workforce and customer identity. Provides employee SSO via OIDC, Active Directory/LDAP integration, adaptive MFA, and lifecycle management. Industry-leading for regulated industries requiring strict access governance; no permanent free tier.

cost.costNotes: Okta Workforce Identity: from $2/user/month (SSO) to $15/user/month (advanced). Customer Identity Cloud: from $150/month for up to 1,000 MAU. No permanent free tier; 30-day trial available.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Okta Workforce Identity starts at $2/user/month for SSO; typical mid-size teams (75 users) = $150/month minimum. Customer Identity (CIAM) starts at $150/month for 1,000 MAU. Max $1,500 reflects a 100-user workforce or 10,000 MAU CIAM deployment.
provenance.adoptionCost: Okta integration requires understanding of Okta admin console, application setup, SAML/OIDC configuration, and optional AD/LDAP connector. Estimated 6 hours at $50/hour = $300 with AI coding assistance.
provenance.licenseCost: No upfront license fee; subscription pricing.

provides.reasoning: Okta is an enterprise-grade managed identity provider with comprehensive IdP capabilities.
Okta handles authentication via OIDC, SAML, OAuth2, and passwordless flows.
Okta is industry-leading for enterprise SSO across web, mobile, and legacy applications.
Okta Adaptive MFA supports TOTP, SMS, Okta Verify, biometrics, hardware keys, and risk-based step-up.
Okta Lifecycle Management automates provisioning/deprovisioning across HR systems and applications.
Okta integrates with Active Directory, LDAP, and HR systems (Workday, BambooHR) as authoritative sources.
Okta Customer Identity supports social login; less common in workforce identity deployments.

requires.reasoning: Okta is a cloud SaaS requiring internet access for authentication flows.

availability.reason: Okta is publicly available with trial and paid plans

supports_nfr.reason: Okta is an OAuth2/OIDC identity provider. Specs using other auth mechanisms (mtls, api_key, jwt, saml) do not require an external IdP of this type.
Okta offers 99.99% uptime SLA on paid plans with a mature multi-region infrastructure.
Okta authentication flows involve hosted login pages and token issuance with minimum ~100ms p95 latency.
Okta p99 latency is ~250ms due to adaptive MFA evaluation and policy engine processing.
Okta is a managed SaaS; RTO depends on Okta infrastructure availability, not application rollback.
Okta supports HIPAA with a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) available on appropriate plans.
Okta is GDPR-compliant with DPA available, EU data residency options, and data deletion APIs.
Okta supports CCPA with data subject request handling and privacy controls.
Okta SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certifications support SOX compliance audit requirements.

supports_constraints.reason: Okta is a cloud-agnostic SaaS that integrates with any cloud or on-premises deployment via standard OAuth2/OIDC/SAML.
Okta provides SDKs for major languages and works with any backend via standard OAuth2/OIDC/SAML protocols.
Okta supports web (Sign-In Widget), mobile (native SDKs), and API authorization (OAuth2 M2M).
Pattern requires Okta as the identity provider.
Okta supports multi-tenancy through Organizations and application-level tenant isolation.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to caching requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to async messaging requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to AI inference requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to batch processing requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to distributed transaction requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to real-time streaming requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to vector search requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to document store requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to key-value store requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to graph database requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to time-series database requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to OLTP workload requirements.
Identity provider pattern is orthogonal to OLAP workload requirements.

identity_type.description: Workforce identity for employee/contractor auth (AD/LDAP integration, lifecycle management). Customer identity (CIAM) for end-user authentication with social login and self-service registration.

mfa_policy.description: MFA enforcement policy. adaptive uses Okta risk engine to step up auth only when risk signals detected. required enforces MFA for all logins.

session_lifetime_hours.description: IdP session lifetime in hours. Workforce typically 8h (work day). Consumer apps often 24-168h. Shorter reduces risk of stolen session tokens.

saml_enabled.description: Enable SAML 2.0 federation in addition to OIDC. Required for integrating legacy enterprise applications that only support SAML (e.g., some ERP, HR systems).
